summary: Iteration 5 testing complete. Backend: 21/22 new tests pass; 1 failure on GET /api/payments/status/{session_id} which returns 500 when Stripe cannot retrieve the session (bug). Places autocomplete, confirmation flow (TURN-XXXXXX, idempotent, sets quote_amount), public booking sanitized payload, Settings (default + 25% propagation), refund-unpaid 400, refund auth, regression all green. Frontend: home loads, navbar/footer phone (650) 410-0687 with tel:+16504100687, Booking pickup PlacesAutocomplete shows dropdown with predictions and updates input on click, Admin Settings tab persists deposit_percent=25 across reload (reset to 100), Payment column shows badges per booking, confirmation_number displayed under status, /pay/{id} renders summary/amount/Pay button and POST /api/payments/checkout returns 200 redirecting to Stripe Checkout (US$120.60 verified).

critical.issue: Returns 500 when emergentintegrations checkout.get_checkout_status raises (Stripe 'No such checkout.session'). The spec requires this endpoint to respond without 500 even if Stripe lookup fails. Wrap the get_checkout_status call in try/except and degrade gracefully — return last-known txn.status (e.g. 'pending') with booking fields rather than 500.

action_items: Fix GET /api/payments/status/{session_id} to not 500 when Stripe API returns CheckoutError. Wrap checkout.get_checkout_status in try/except and fall back to txn.status from DB. Reproduction: create checkout with platform key sk_test_emergent → call /api/payments/status/<sid> → 500 with traceback 'No such checkout.session'.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py line ~658 — get_payment_status lets emergentintegrations CheckoutError bubble up as unhandled 500. Should be wrapped to satisfy contract.

places_autocomplete returns shape {predictions:[], status: data.status} on success but {predictions:[], error: ...} on failure / no key. Inconsistent — consider always including a 'status' field for the FE to inspect.
/api/bookings/{id}/public has no rate-limit / id-guessing protection. UUIDs are not enumerable but consider logging/abuse limits since it leaks PII (full_name, email, phone-less but still email).

RefundRequest path — when Stripe REST API is called with the platform-shared sk_test_emergent it will likely return 401/403; surface a clearer 502 message rather than passing the raw Stripe text. (Out of scope until live key tested.)

_compute_quote_amount is invoked from both /public and /payments/checkout. On a call_only vehicle it returns None silently which is fine, but for a priced vehicle whose geocode fails the customer would see deposit_amount=null on the pay page with no message. Consider returning a structured 'quote_unavailable' state for the public endpoint.
PayBooking.jsx: confirmation_number is rendered but the test that ran during redirect could not verify because navigation to Stripe started immediately. Functionally correct.

success_rate.frontend: 100% (verified flows: places autocomplete, settings tab, payment column, /pay page checkout redirect, navbar/footer phone)

seed_data_creation: TEST_-prefixed bookings created/deleted within tests. Settings deposit_percent reset to 100 at end of suite (verified). One temporary booking 86ecc21d-... created during UI test was deleted.
context_for_next_testing_agent: All Iteration 5 tests live in /app/backend/tests/test_iteration5.py (22 cases). The single failing test is TestPayments::test_checkout_creates_session — the create succeeds and booking flips to payment_status='pending', but GET /api/payments/status/{sid} returns 500 because emergentintegrations StripeCheckout.get_checkout_status raises CheckoutError when Stripe responds 'No such checkout.session'. Fix is to catch CheckoutError in the handler and return PaymentStatus with txn.status fallback. After fix, re-run the full /app/backend/tests/test_iteration5.py and /app/backend/tests/test_turonlimo_api.py suites. Verified flows still working: admin login, options endpoint, S-Class only (no Luxury Sedan), pricing CRUD, quote endpoint, contacts CRUD.
rca of the issue: RCA — /api/payments/status/{session_id} 500: handler at server.py:658 invokes `await checkout.get_checkout_status(session_id)` without exception handling. emergentintegrations re-raises stripe._error.InvalidRequestError as CheckoutError. With sk_test_emergent (a shared platform-style test key) freshly created session IDs are sometimes not retrievable on the read API path even though create succeeded — this surfaces during normal customer polling and is reproducible 100% in this env. Mitigation: wrap the call, on CheckoutError log warning and respond with payment_status=txn.status (likely 'initiated' or 'pending'), booking_status from DB, amount/currency from txn record, confirmation_number from booking. This satisfies the contract 'endpoint responds without 500' even when Stripe is temporarily unavailable.
